content.problemStatement: Write a C program that swaps the values of two integer variables, a =5 and b = 10, and prints their new values using a single printf statement with a space between them
constraints: No input. Output is always fixed.
The output must be two integers separated by a space.
content.outputFormat: Print two space-separated integers on a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Swapping the values of a and b
Same output as sample_1 since input is empty
Same output as sample_1 and sample_2 since input is empty

solution.approach: The problem requires swapping the values of two integer variables and printing their new values. This can be achieved by using a temporary variable to hold one of the values while swapping. The algorithm involves assigning the value of the first variable to the temporary variable, then assigning the value of the second variable to the first variable, and finally assigning the value of the temporary variable to the second variable.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) because the algorithm involves a constant number of operations regardless of the input size.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) because the algorithm uses a constant amount of space to store the temporary variable.

code.Python3:
a = 5
b = 10
temp = a
a = b
b = temp
print(a, b)
